channel.title: Software Quality on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Software Quality on CEDIS

item.title: Products
item.description: Web application developed as a bachelor’s thesis with academic support from CEDIS for gamified learning about code smells, combining conceptual guides, interactive exercises, hints, treats, statistics, and progression.

item.title: Software Quality
item.description: Constitutes a fundamental pillar in software engineering, directly reflecting on user satisfaction, operational efficiency, and the sustainability of computer systems.

item.title: People
item.description: Adjunct Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program on software engineering, software metrics, testing, and software quality.

item.title: People
item.description: Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the Software Engineering undergraduate program on software quality, digital government, and software engineering education.

item.title: Paper on DevDog accepted at the SBES 2026 Tools Track
item.description: A paper stemming from Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior's bachelor's thesis was accepted at SBES 2026, supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas and co-supervised by Prof. André Lanna.

item.title: DevDog: undergraduate thesis turns code smells into gamified learning
item.description: Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that gave rise to DevDog, a gamified web application for teaching code smell identification.

item.title: Gamifying Scrum: an undergraduate thesis maps the literature and drafts guidelines for the field
item.description: Denniel Roriz Lima defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis running a bibliometric and thematic review on the use of gamification in agile processes with Scrum, and proposing practical guidelines for implementation.

item.title: Accessibility tools and an ageing Brazil: an undergraduate thesis compares what the market offers
item.description: Daniel Barcelos Moreira and Juliana Pereira Valle Gonçalves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis evaluating tools used to measure the accessibility of web sites used by elderly people.

item.title: Continuous improvement focused on usability: an undergraduate thesis applies established mechanisms in a web app
item.description: Francisco Heronildo Sousa Santos and João Vitor Ferreira Alves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying established usability mechanisms in an evaluation-and-improvement cycle on a web application.

item.title: Accessibility metrics for visually impaired users: an undergraduate thesis distills audit practice
item.description: Ricardo de Castro Loureiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing metrics to evaluate the accessibility of web pages for people with visual impairment — a direct step toward more objective audit practices.

item.title: LGPD inside the app: an undergraduate thesis turns privacy requirements into code
item.description: Lucas Lopes Xavier defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis translating LGPD and ISO 29100 requirements into implementable specifications for applications — shortening the distance between legal norms and engineering practice.

item.title: Software quality and an ageing Brazil: an undergraduate thesis reads TIC Household data
item.description: Guilherme Daniel Fernandes da Silva and Lorrayne Alves Cardozo defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis analyzing the relationship between software quality and population ageing using data from Brazil’s TIC Household survey.

item.title: Police intelligence reports under the gamification lens: a dissertation measures what changes
item.description: Públio Pastrolin Cavalcante defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation evaluating how gamification applied to police intelligence activities affects the quality of reports produced.

item.title: Why don't companies use processes they already paid to define?
item.description: Nathalia Lorena Cardoso Dias defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis using a systematic literature review to investigate why formally defined software improvement processes are frequently unused in practice.

item.title: Ia qvt an accessible tool to measure quality of work life
item.description: Gabriel de Jesus Carvalho defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing IA-QVT — a tool to collect and process data on quality of work life, with strong attention to accessibility.

item.title: University of Heuristics: an undergraduate thesis turns HCI teaching into a serious game
item.description: Cauê Mateus Oliveira and Lucas Midlhey Cardoso Naves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis turning the study of Nielsen’s heuristics into a game — the University of Heuristics.

item.title: What users say when no one asks: an undergraduate thesis mines Play Store reviews for usability signals
item.description: Augusto Moreno Vilarins Cardoso da Silva and Ícaro Pereira de Oliveira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing a semi-automated process to extract usability signals from user comments on the Google Play Store.

item.title: Interfaces that induce error: an undergraduate thesis catalogues common traps — and how to avoid them
item.description: Caio César de Almeida Beleza defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis analyzing software interface problems that push users toward error and systematizing recommendations to avoid them.

item.title: Software process improvement: where to start when everything seems chaotic?
item.description: Rafael Contessotto Bragança Pinheiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing an approach to help organizations kick off software process improvement programs — the hardest step when almost everything demands attention.

item.title: Exploratory testing in action: an undergraduate thesis pairs scripted and free approaches to evaluate software
item.description: Iolane Caroline Alves de Andrade defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying exploratory testing alongside traditional testing, highlighting the learning gain that exploratory testing brings to the process.

item.title: Refactoring as leverage: an undergraduate thesis shows how to rewrite code for variability
item.description: Ronyell Henrique dos Santos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying refactoring operations to improve software system variability — reducing maintenance costs over the lifecycle.

item.title: SOA, agile, and QA in the same case study: a dissertation documents where three cultures actually meet
item.description: James Taylor Faria Chaves defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation analyzing, through a case study, the encounter between Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), agile development methods, and Quality Assurance practices.

item.title: Making the invisible visible: an undergraduate thesis proposes a method to expose technical debt in agile teams
item.description: Iago Rodrigues Gonçalves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating how to identify, measure and communicate technical debt — a problem that gains weight in agile methodologies focused on continuous delivery.

item.title: Milliseconds matter: an undergraduate thesis measures the impact of code choices on Android
item.description: Victor Hugo Alves de Carvalho defended in 2015 at FCTE/UnB an empirical undergraduate thesis comparing programming techniques on Android and measuring the effect of each on app performance and responsiveness.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Conference by Bruno Mello Andrade, Sergio Antônio Andrade de Freitas (2026).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Paper accepted at the Tools Track of the 40th Brazilian Symposium on Software Engineering (SBES 2026), presenting DevDog, a deployed gamified web platform with theory guides, contextualized exercises, line-level smell marking, immediate feedback, collectible treats (petiscos), progressive hints, and community statistics. Implemented with React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Express, Prisma, and PostgreSQL, covering a catalog of 19 smell types inspired by Fowler’s catalog.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Article by Ricardo Ajax Dias Kosloski, Leides Barroso de Azevedo Moura, Marília Miranda Forte Gomes (2026).

item.description: Tcc by Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior (2026/1).

item.title: Doarti
item.description: Project for a technological solution to strengthen donation processes in the Federal District.

item.description: Tcc by Iolane Caroline Alves de Andrade (2020).

item.description: Conference by Angélica Toffano Seidel Calazans, Roberto Ávila Paldês, Edna Dias Canedo, Eloisa Toffano Seidel Masson, Fernando de Albuquerque Guimarães, Kiane Mabel Fialho Rezende, Emeli Braosi, Ricardo Ajax Dias Kosloski (2019).
